fundamentals.name: MP Materials Corp.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: MP Materials Corp., together with its subsidiaries, produces rare earth materials in the Western Hemisphere. It operates in two segments, Materials and Magnetics. The Materials segment owns and operates the Mountain Pass Rare Earth Mine and Processing facility located near Mountain Pass, San Bernardino County, California. The Magnetics segment produces magnetic precursor products, including NdPr metal; and manufactures NdFeB permanent magnets. MP Materials Corp. was founded in 2017 and is headquartered in Las Vegas, Nevada.

judgment.summary: High-growth potential but currently loss-making with negative margins and cash flow, trading at a high forward P/E.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates with EPS of $0.03 vs. $-0.04 (surprise 174.6%); next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 118.6% YoY — significant top-line expansion.
Last quarter EPS surprise of 174.6% — beat expectations significantly.
Total cash $1.74B provides a substantial liquidity buffer.
Analyst consensus is strong buy with a mean target of $80.40.
negatives: Operating margin -7.9% and profit margin -20.5% — company is not profitable on an operating basis.
Free cash flow (TTM) is negative $-174.84M — burning cash.
Forward P/E of 55.97 and Price/Sales of 33.17 indicate a very high valuation.
Return on equity and assets are negative, indicating poor capital efficiency.

news.paragraph: MP Materials stock has seen recent downward pressure despite a partnership with Apple, with articles highlighting concerns around valuation, insider sales, and geopolitical factors. While the company reported a strong earnings surprise last quarter, its operating and profit margins remain negative, and free cash flow is also negative. Analysts maintain a strong buy consensus with a mean target of $80.40, suggesting potential upside despite recent headwinds.
